interaction.prompt: PK. They've gained the zone and the puck is on the strong-side wall. As F1, tap which opposing player you should pressure first.

feedback.right: Pressure the puck-carrier. F2 stays in the slot to take away the bumper; the D's have the points and weak-side. Always close on the puck first.
feedback.wrong: If F1 cheats off the carrier, the PP gets a free shot or pass. Always close on the PUCK first — the box rotates around your pressure.

tip: PK rule #1: F1 pressures the puck. F2 reads off you. The Ds hold the box. Don't overthink it.
why: Penalty kill is about shrinking time and space. The puck-carrier is the most dangerous player by definition — get a stick + body on him so the rest of the kill can read off your pressure.
